description: Unified, machine-readable ground-truth across all four Colima Desktop frontends. Embeds per-platform summaries and a canonical surface matrix. Raw element arrays live in the per-platform source files referenced below.

macos.capture_method: System Events entire-contents traversal (12 surfaces); Peekaboo AX snapshot fallback (Configuration — System Events timed out on large tree)
macos.capture_environment: macOS 26.5.2, aarch64, AX+screen-recording grants, real colima backend (RealServiceProvider, profile=default)
macos.verified_status: VERIFIED — zero capture errors, 13 distinct tab fingerprints, real AX elements from live app

windows.capture_method: FlaUI 4.0.0 / UIA3 (UIAutomationClient COM); Click-first navigation; Dashboard uses no-navigation (initial selected page); mainWindow re-resolved per iteration; navAutomationId eagerly captured before scroll
windows.capture_environment: GitHub Actions runner, Windows NT 10.0.26100.0 (x64), unpackaged WinUI 3 (WindowsPackageType=None, WindowsAppSDKSelfContained=true), no live daemon
windows.verified_status: VERIFIED — zero capture_errors, 13 distinct surfaces, all element_counts nonzero; daemon not running in CI (gRPC surfaces show placeholder/empty data)

linux.capture_method: pyatspi DFS traversal (read-only) + screenshots; sidebar navigation: Dashboard initial state + xdotool positional grid (x=win_x+100, y=win_y+23+38*i) for surfaces 1–11; each navigation verified by content fingerprint change; all 12 content fingerprints unique. GTK_A11Y=atspi. DISC-03 resolved: Monitoring added as 12th surface (index 11).
linux.capture_environment: GitHub Actions runner, Ubuntu, Xvfb :99, no live daemon; colima presence shimmed by CI-only scripts/linux/colima_shim.sh so main window loads instead of onboarding
linux.verified_status: VERIFIED — 12 surfaces, 12 unique content fingerprints, all element_counts nonzero, zero errors; daemon not running (backend-unavailable surfaces show stub/empty data)

tui.capture_method: hybrid: PTY screenshots + model-direct content (fakeDS). PTY frames captured by headless terminal driver (tui/driver_explore.go); content fingerprints unique per surface. model-direct: fakeDS DataSource injects stub data to populate normally-empty views when daemon is unreachable. DISC-03 resolved: Monitoring added as 12th surface.
tui.capture_environment: GitHub Actions runner, linux/amd64, Go 1.25.0, 120×40 PTY, no live daemon; fakeDS provides plausible stub content for surfaces that would otherwise show only error text
tui.verified_status: VERIFIED — 12 surfaces, all_nonempty=true, all_distinct=true, validation_pass=true, zero errors; content is structurally correct but DATA IS STUB (fakeDS) — does not represent live daemon state

macos.backend_connection: Direct: colima CLI + Docker socket (RealServiceProvider); no gRPC required
capture_limitations: Configuration surface used Peekaboo fallback (System Events timed out on 448-element tree)
Some AX identifiers show 'missing value' — SwiftUI accessibility annotations incomplete for non-interactive layout elements
macos.backend_note: Ran against real colima 0.10.1, profile=default; VM status was live at capture time

windows.backend_connection: gRPC over TCP (DaemonClient.cs); CI runner had no live daemon
capture_limitations: No live daemon in CI — gRPC-backed surfaces (containers, images, volumes, networks, machines, profiles) show empty/placeholder data
NavigationView Click-first navigation was required; SelectionItemPattern.Select() did not trigger frame changes in WinUI 3
Unpackaged WinUI 3 exe (no MSIX) — some automation IDs may differ from packaged build
windows.backend_note: Daemon not running; element counts reflect UI chrome only, not live data rows

linux.backend_connection: gRPC over Unix socket (client.rs); CI runner had no live daemon; colima shimmed
capture_limitations: xdotool positional grid navigation: clicks are coordinate-based (not AT-SPI widget refs) — accurate for fixed sidebar grid but brittle if sidebar row height changes
AT-SPI doAction/component-extent navigation removed (pass 8) after proving it produced mislabeled captures — xdotool grid is the authoritative method
No live daemon — gRPC-backed data surfaces show error/empty content (AT-SPI element_names_fingerprint still shows widget names, not data)
colima presence shimmed via scripts/linux/colima_shim.sh to avoid onboarding screen
linux.backend_note: Daemon not running; '✗ Error: transport error' visible in dashboard fingerprint — UI chrome and widget labels verified, not live data

tui.backend_connection: gRPC over Unix socket; headless PTY driver (driver_explore.go); fakeDS DataSource for stub content
capture_limitations: STUB DATA: fakeDS DataSource injects mock container/image/volume/network/profile/machine rows — these are not live daemon responses; purpose is to confirm surfaces render populated tables, not to validate backend data
TUI navigation is keyboard-driven (1–0 number keys + Machines + Monitoring); uniqueness validated by content fingerprint diff, not AT-SPI/UIA widget inspection
No accessibility tree — PTY character grids are the only introspection method for terminal apps
tui.backend_note: fakeDS provided stub data; real daemon was unreachable (unix socket absent); status bar shows 'daemon unreachable' on live-data surfaces, stub rows on others

canonical_surface_matrix.description: Normalized mapping of every surface across all four platforms. Names are lowercased/de-spaced canonical keys. 'captured' = surface appears in ground-truth with nonzero element count. 'backend_dependent' = data rows require a live daemon/colima to show real content. Source-aligned labels provided per platform.
canonical_surface_matrix.common_surface_note: 12 surfaces are present across all four platforms (runtime-confirmed). Two additional surfaces are platform-specific extras.

surfaces.notes: VM status, CPU/mem/disk stats, start/stop/restart buttons. All 4 platforms render. macOS shows live colima status; Windows/Linux/TUI show error/stub.
Docker container list + CRUD. All 4 platforms captured. Live data on macOS only; TUI shows fakeDS stub rows.
Docker image list + pull/remove/push. All 4 platforms captured.
Docker volume list + CRUD. All 4 platforms captured.
Docker network list + connect/disconnect. All 4 platforms captured.
CPU/mem/disk/arch/runtime/network/kubernetes config form. macOS has 448 AX elements (richest); Windows 69, Linux 78, TUI renders a config form.
Lima VM list (limactl list). All 4 platforms captured. Live data on macOS only.
Colima profile list + create/delete/clone. All 4 platforms captured. TUI shows fakeDS stub profiles.
AI model list/pull/run/serve. All 4 platforms captured. macOS uses tab label 'ai'; Windows 'aiworkloads'; Linux 'AI Workloads'; TUI 'AI Workloads'. Normalized as ai_workloads.

Runtime switch (docker/containerd/incus) + update. macOS uses tab key 'runtimeControls' (181 AX elements — richest). All 4 platforms have this surface. DISCREPANCY: macOS tab label is 'runtimeControls'; other platforms use 'runtime' or 'Runtime'.

VMStats stream, process list, kill. PRESENT on all 4 platforms. macOS 111 elem, Windows 47 elem, Linux 76 elem (AT-SPI verified: CPU/Memory/Disk usage, Process list, PID kill), TUI renders VM Monitoring frame with CPU/Memory/Disk stats and process kill. DISC-03 RESOLVED.
macOS-only Community/Help tab (143 AX elements). Not implemented on Windows, Linux, or TUI. Not required by CONTRACT — macOS-specific.
Windows-only Settings page (69 UIA elements). Not present in macOS, Linux, or TUI. Not required by CONTRACT — Windows-specific.

active.finding: macOS tab key is 'runtimeControls' (SwiftUI NavigationItem enum value); Windows/Linux/TUI all use 'runtime'/'Runtime'. AX element count on macOS is 181 vs 42–68 on other platforms, indicating a richer UI (update button, runtime detail cards). Runtime capability parity exists but the surface name differs.
active.action: Informational — no contract violation. Consider aligning tab key to 'runtime' in macOS NavigationItem if cross-platform automation is needed.

active.finding: Surface is named 'ai' (tab key) in macOS, 'aiworkloads' in Windows, 'AI Workloads' in Linux, 'AI Workloads' in TUI. All four have captured the surface; naming is cosmetically inconsistent but functionally equivalent.
active.action: Informational — recommend canonical label 'AI Workloads' / key 'ai_workloads' across all platforms.

active.finding: Community tab is macOS-only (143 AX elements). Not required by CONTRACT. No action needed.
Settings page is Windows-only (69 UIA elements). Not in CONTRACT, not required. Reasonable for platform-specific connection/dependency configuration.
active.action: None — Windows-specific extra surface. Consider whether Linux and macOS need equivalent settings.

active.finding: Windows, Linux, and TUI captures were taken without a live daemon. Backend-dependent surfaces (containers, images, volumes, networks, machines, profiles, dashboard VM stats, kubernetes status) show placeholder/empty/error content. This is expected for CI captures; it does NOT indicate a UI bug — it confirms the daemon connection path exists.
active.action: Real-backend interaction coverage requires a live colima environment. macOS is the only platform with live-backend ground truth.

active.finding: TUI capture_method is 'hybrid: PTY screenshots + model-direct content (fakeDS)'. Stub container/image/profile/machine rows were injected by fakeDS DataSource to produce non-empty table renders. These are NOT live daemon responses. TUI surface structure is verified; data accuracy is not.
active.action: TUI data-layer testing requires a live daemon connection. fakeDS fulfills structural verification only.

resolved.resolution: RESOLVED — Monitoring surface implemented in Linux (GTK4) and TUI (Bubble Tea). Linux ground-truth now shows 12 surfaces with Monitoring at 76 AT-SPI elements (CPU/Memory/Disk usage, Process list, PID kill). TUI ground-truth shows 12 surfaces with Monitoring (unique fingerprint 426c0fa142250c03, VM Monitoring frame with stats + kill). Both verified in fresh CI captures (2026-07-18T13:10Z Linux, 2026-07-18T13:16Z TUI).
resolved.original_finding: Monitoring surface was absent from Linux and TUI ground-truth (11 surfaces each). VMStats stream + ProcessList + KillProcess RPCs existed in daemon but were not wired in linux/src/views/ or tui/internal/ui/.

explicit_limitations: macOS is the ONLY platform with a live-backend capture (real colima 0.10.1, real Docker socket, live containers). Element counts on macOS reflect real populated data rows.
Windows (CI), Linux (CI), and TUI captures ran without a live daemon. Element counts reflect UI chrome and widget structure only — data rows are absent, stubbed (TUI fakeDS), or showing error text.
TUI has no accessibility tree — PTY character grids are the only introspection method. Semantic element roles, automation IDs, and interactive element boundaries are not capturable.

Linux navigation uses xdotool positional grid (coordinate-based); accurate for fixed sidebar layout but not self-describing — sidebar row indices are positionally inferred, not accessibility-label-verified.
macOS 'community' and Windows 'settings' are platform-specific extras not required by CONTRACT v1.
Template editing (GetTemplate/SetTemplate RPCs) is not surfaced in any platform UI — only configuration YAML (GetConfig/SetConfig) is accessible.
DependencyManager (Part C, M4.13) is wired on macOS and Windows (DependencyManager.cs) but not verified via runtime interaction.
Windows capture is from GitHub Actions runner, which may have display scaling, DPI, or COM marshaling differences from a real developer machine.
Linux capture uses colima_shim.sh to bypass onboarding; the real app onboarding flow (DependencyManager::is_colima_installed = false) is not captured.

total_elements_captured.TUI: 12 PTY frames (no element count; character-grid introspection)

unique_surface_fingerprints.macOS: not computed (verified by AX traversal, each tab independently navigated)
unique_surface_fingerprints.Windows: not computed (13 distinct UIA trees verified)

gaps_requiring_action: DISC-07: TUI data layer unverified (fakeDS stub) — real-backend test needed
DISC-06: Windows/Linux backend-connected verification deferred to live environments
